entries.session_goal: Bootstrap lock-file workflow: CLAUDE.md files, roadmap, all lock files, all pre-writable specs
decisions: Adopted advisor/executor separation via lock-file workflow skill v3.0
Confirmed PRD build order as fixed: F1+F2 → U1 → N1/N2/N3 → E1/E2 → A1/A2 → N4/S1
Confirmed all PRD architecture decisions as locked
Established five lock file domains: architecture, ui-components, data-model, export-formats, platform-adapters
Bundled F1 and F2 into single executor session (F2 is trivial after F1)
Chose in-place vertical expansion (80px wide, grows downward) for U1 — no layout refactor
Canvas notes (N1) stored in cttv-canvas-${conversationId}; color key in global cttv-colorkey
E1 exports annotated turns only (markdown); E2 exports full tree (Obsidian Canvas JSON)
A1, A2, N4, S1 specs NOT written — require DOM analysis or design sessions first
Phase 1 (F1+F2) activated in current-phase/ — ready for executor session

entries.session_goal: Triage post-Phase-7 UX issues; write Phase 8 and Phase 9 specs; update locks and roadmap; activate Phase 8
decisions: Pin-on-click mechanic removed entirely — click always navigates (Phase 8)
Node text always visible with -webkit-line-clamp:2 default, not toggled by JS (Phase 8)
Hover expansion width increased from 80px to 220px (Phase 8)
Expansion class-based via .cttv-node-text-expanded, not inline style toggle (Phase 8)
refreshNode() height:100% bug fixed to min-height:100%;height:auto (Phase 8)
Tool node text excluded from chainTexts — only assistant nodes push display text (Phase 9)
selectionchange listener added to fix drag-to-note reliability (Phase 9 — overlaps Phase 5 intent)
Sticky notes gain resize:both + overflow:hidden + mouseup dimension save (Phase 9)
StickyNote schema extended with height?: number field (LOCK-data-model v1.1)
LOCK-ui-components updated to v1.1 reflecting Phase 8 node expansion design
ROADMAP updated: old Phases 8-11 renumbered to 10-13; new Phases 8-9 inserted
Phase 8 activated in current-phase/ (SPEC + LOCK-architecture + LOCK-ui-components)

entries.session_goal: Triage post-Phase-9 runtime errors; design and write Phase 10 (node width + note satellites)
decisions: e.target.closest crash confirmed — Phase 9 executor ran before spec update; fix carried into Phase 10
Node width changed to permanent 220px — hover expands height only, no width animation (Phase 10)
NODE_STRIDE_X changed to 480 to reserve satellite space: node(220) + sat-gap(20) + satellite(220) + col-gap(20)
Obsidian Canvas export x-coordinate decoupled from NODE_STRIDE_X — now fixed at col*300
Note dot (.cttv-note-dot) replaced by satellite note nodes — same shape as main node, positioned to the right
Satellite connected by short horizontal SVG line (stroke #88ccff)
Satellite hover: height expands to 260px, shows full note with scroll (same mechanic as main nodes)
Satellite click: opens notes dialog for that node (openNotesDialog() helper extracted from menu handler)
Satellite managed by renderMap() on initial render and refreshNode() on incremental note changes
LOCK-ui-components updated to v1.2 with satellite constants and behavior
Single source of truth for lock files confirmed: all 5 live in current-phase/ only
Lock files now persisted across phase activations — only spec file is replaced per phase

entries.session_goal: Fix three satellite note bugs discovered during live testing: note not appearing on create, notes gated on star/color, no delete button on satellite notes
decisions: refreshNode(id) was never called after cttv-notes-save — satellite note element never created until re-render; fixed by adding refreshNode(id) after saveAnnotations() in save handler
Notes are structurally independent of star/color in refreshNode already — root cause was missing refreshNode call, not conditional gating
Satellite notes gain an × delete button (reuses .cttv-sticky-delete style) — click removes element, edge, clears ann.notes, saves
justify-content overridden to flex-start on satellite to accommodate delete button above text
NOTE: this session bypassed advisor/executor separation — fixes implemented directly without a spec; no spec file written

entries.session_goal: Fix satellite note visibility bug, add satellite delete button, diagnose WSL2 dev environment issue, improve Obsidian Canvas export fidelity
decisions: Root cause of note not appearing: refreshNode(id) was never called from cttv-notes-save handler — data was saved but DOM never updated
Satellite × button added to createSatelliteEl() — matches sticky note pattern, click removes element + edge + annotation + saves
WSL2 Windows Explorer file caching diagnosed: Explorer reads stale bytes when copying from \\wsl$\ path; fix is to point Chrome directly at WSL repo via Load Unpacked
Canvas export: stars now export as ⭐ text prefix + yellow background ('3') for starred-only nodes
Canvas export: unimportant nodes export as 👁 text prefix
Canvas export: notes now separate satellite nodes (color '5') to the right with right→left edge, replacing inline text append
Canvas export: sticky notes exported as floating yellow nodes below tree in 4-column grid
Canvas export: col stride widened 300→500 to prevent satellite overlap with adjacent column nodes
Color key canvas export identified as missing — deferred to next session
docs/ folder created with debugging-lessons.md documenting root causes and spec rules

entries.session_goal: Phase 11: two canvas export improvements — unimportant node minimization and full-text export
decisions: Unimportant nodes: remove 👁 prefix; export as 60×30 minimal slivers instead of full-size nodes — full text preserved for recoverability in Obsidian
Full text export: remove truncate(n.text, 300) limit; use n.text directly for all nodes
Dynamic node height: Math.max(NODE_H, Math.min(600, Math.ceil(text.length / 38) * 22 + 24)) — min 100px, max 600px
Satellite notes unchanged — still export at SAT_H = 100 fixed; export for all nodes including unimportant
LOCK-export-formats updated to v1.1 with accurate current constants (STRIDE_X=500, not 300 as v1.0 stated) and Phase 11 rules
truncate() helper in canvas export scope can be removed — no longer called; markdown export has its own copy in a different scope
